evidence: Confirmed mechanical signal in `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/components/MediaGalleryLightbox.tsx`: dead-internal-code pattern exists via `void cacheVersion;` inside `enhancedMedia` memo, indicating API/state synchronization is being forced indirectly instead of through a clear contract.

Confirmed mechanical signal in `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/StrokeOverlay.tsx`: the public prop surface combines overlapping mode controls (`isInpaintMode`, `isAnnotateMode`, `annotationMode`, and `editMode`) plus many behavioral callbacks, which creates invalid-state combinations and mode ambiguity at call boundaries.

Refuted mechanical signal for `src/domains/generation/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts`: current file does not contain public async APIs without awaits; flagged async smell appears stale or non-material for API coherence.

Refuted mechanical signal for `src/integrations/supabase/bootstrap/createSupabaseClient.ts`: the core exported API is synchronous and coherent for bootstrap; detector hit is likely from an internal async callback shape, not a boundary-level contract defect.

Refuted signature-variance signals for `handleKeyDown` and `handleTouchStart/End` in `ProjectSelectorPopover` and `ReferenceThumbnail`: these are local component handlers with React/DOM event-type differences, not shared exported APIs.

Refuted `generateUniqueFilename` variance signal for `src/shared/components/LoraSelectorModal/utils/validation-utils.ts`: that module exports `generateUniqueLoraFilename`; similarly named `generateUniqueFilename` belongs to `src/shared/lib/storagePaths.ts` and is a separate domain utility.
Systemic coherence issue remains in lightbox and upload subsystems: multiple parallel adapter/flat contracts and legacy positional APIs increase indirection and behavior drift risk.

findings.summary: Tool settings read/write APIs use incompatible error/result contracts within one boundary, creating adapter churn and inconsistent caller behavior.
findings.suggestion: Standardize settings service APIs to one failure contract (either all `OperationResult` or all throw typed errors). Prefer a single boundary adapter so hooks/prefetch paths do not need per-call conversion logic.
evidence: `fetchToolSettingsSupabase` returns `Promise<OperationResult<SettingsFetchResult>>` and callers must unwrap `.ok`/`.value` (or convert to throw).
`updateToolSettingsSupabase` returns a promise from `enqueueSettingsWrite` that rejects on failure (no `OperationResult` wrapper).

Callers use both patterns in the same feature area (`toToolSettingsErrorFromOperationFailure`, `classifyToolSettingsError`, direct thrown errors), indicating a split API contract instead of one coherent surface.

findings.summary: Identically named `stripModeFromPhaseConfig` APIs disagree on return type, and one signature lies about preserving `mode`.
findings.suggestion: Unify to one exported strip helper and one return type (`Omit<PhaseConfig, 'mode'>`), then update call sites to consume the narrowed request shape explicitly.
evidence: `segmentSettingsUtils.stripModeFromPhaseConfig` returns `PhaseConfig` but destructures out `mode` and returns `rest`, so runtime shape omits a declared field.
`modelPhase.stripModeFromPhaseConfig` models the same behavior as `Omit<PhaseConfig, 'mode'>` (`PhaseConfigRequest`), which is the honest contract.
Both helpers are used in travel/video generation pathways, so consumers see two conflicting API shapes for the same operation.

findings.summary: `refetchTimelineFrameCaches` is a synchronous `void` API despite performing async refetch work, unlike neighboring mutation APIs.
findings.suggestion: Return `Promise<void>` from `refetchTimelineFrameCaches` and `await Promise.all([...refetchQueries])` at call sites where post-refetch ordering matters.
evidence: `refetchTimelineFrameCaches` calls `queryClient.refetchQueries(...)` multiple times but returns `void` and does not await or expose completion.
In `useTimelineFrameUpdates`, it is called after persisted writes as if refetch is part of mutation completion flow, but no completion signal exists.
Same module exports async mutation helpers (`persistSingleTimelineFrame`, `syncTimelineGenerationFrames`) that are awaited, making this one API semantically inconsistent.

findings.summary: Project-scoped reference persistence accepts undefined project ID and still attempts project-scope writes.
findings.suggestion: Make `persistReferenceSelection` require `selectedProjectId: string` for project-scope writes, or add explicit fallback behavior (`OperationFailure` with `invalid_scope_identifier`) before cache/write operations when project is missing.
evidence: `persistReferenceSelection` takes `selectedProjectId: string | undefined` and always calls `updateProjectImageSettings('project', ...)` without guarding missing project scope.
Both `useStyleReferenceUploadHandler` and `useResourceSelectHandler` pass through possibly undefined `selectedProjectId` directly to `persistReferenceSelection`.
Cache access also keys project settings with `settingsQueryKeys.tool(..., selectedProjectId, undefined)` when project may be undefined, blending project-intent APIs with unscoped keys.

findings.summary: Media lightbox exposes two parallel API surfaces (flat props and grouped adapter), causing contract duplication and drift risk.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one canonical public contract for lightbox boundaries (prefer grouped domain contracts), and generate any compatibility mapping in one place with compile-time exhaustiveness checks; remove duplicate flat/grouped definitions.
evidence: `MediaLightboxProps` defines a large flat surface while `MediaLightboxInputAdapter` re-groups nearly the same fields into `core/navigation/shotWorkflow/actions/...` and then flattens them again in `toMediaLightboxProps`.
`MediaGalleryLightbox` must construct a deep adapter object and maintain conversion logic for values like `isDeleting`, showing boundary indirection rather than a single stable contract.
The same behavior fields (`onAddToShot`, navigation handlers, feature flags) are repeated across multiple interfaces, increasing mismatch risk when one side evolves.

findings.summary: Deletion status uses incompatible union semantics across lightbox boundaries (`boolean|string|null` vs `string|null`), forcing ad-hoc coercion.
findings.suggestion: Replace `isDeleting` unions with a discriminated status contract (for example `{ kind: 'idle' } | { kind: 'pendingAny' } | { kind: 'pendingId', id: string }`) and propagate it consistently through adapter and lightbox types.
evidence: `MediaGalleryLightboxActionContract` accepts `isDeleting?: string | boolean | null`, mixing 'any deletion in progress' and 'specific id deleting' semantics.
Downstream lightbox action types normalize `isDeleting` to `string | null`, so `MediaGalleryLightbox` must coerce boolean into an id-like value (`resolvedDeletingId`).
This loses semantic precision and can disable controls based on approximated state rather than explicit deletion target identity.

findings.summary: Image and video upload APIs in shared lib have diverged into incompatible signatures, leaving legacy positional parameters in active use.
findings.suggestion: Create a single upload contract shape for media (`uploadMediaToStorage` options object or aligned `uploadVideoToStorage` v2), deprecate positional args with a typed migration shim, and update call sites in one refactor pass.
evidence: `uploadImageToStorage` uses `(file, maxRetriesOrOptions?, onProgress?)` with an options-object path.
`uploadVideoToStorage` still requires deprecated positional `_projectId` and `_shotId` args (unused internally) plus mixed options handling.

Call sites still pass project/shot positional args (`uploadVideoToStorage(file, projectId!, shotId)`), preserving an API shape that no longer matches behavior and differs from image upload conventions.
